---
title: Econ490_Analysis_2
date: '2024'
description: NA
categories:
  - Python
format:
  html: default
  ipynb:
    jupyter:
      kernelspec:
        display_name: Python
        language: python3
        name: python3
jupyter:
  jupytext:
    text_representation:
      extension: .qmd
      format_name: quarto
      format_version: '1.0'
      jupytext_version: 1.16.1
  kernelspec:
    display_name: Python 3 (ipykernel)
    language: Python
    name: python3
---

Import dataset 

```{python}

import pandas as pd

# Path to the files
path = r"C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/Final"

# Open the first file
main_data = pd.read_excel(f"{path}/processed_combined_data_1999-2023.xlsx")

# Open the second file
controls_data = pd.read_excel(f"{path}/Controls_Dataset_2.xlsx")

# Print the first few rows of each dataframe to verify they've been loaded correctly
print("First few rows of processed_combined_data_1999-2023.xlsx:")
print(main_data.head())

print("\nFirst few rows of Controls_Dataset_2.xlsx:")
print(controls_data.head())

```

Missing 1999 Premiums both variables per capita graph 

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd

# Path to the file
path = r"C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/Final"

# Read all sheets from the Excel file
excel_file = pd.ExcelFile(f"{path}/processed_combined_data_1999-2023.xlsx")

# Initialize lists to store results
years = range(2000, 2024)
premium_means = []
charges_means = []

# Loop through all sheets and collect means, skipping the first sheet (1999)
for sheet_name in excel_file.sheet_names[1:]:  # Start from the second sheet
    df = pd.read_excel(excel_file, sheet_name=sheet_name)
    
    if 'WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA' in df.columns:
        premium_means.append(df['WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA'].mean())
    
    if 'WLFR_TOT_CHARGES_PAID_AMT_PER_CAPITA' in df.columns:
        charges_means.append(df['WLFR_TOT_CHARGES_PAID_AMT_PER_CAPITA'].mean())

# Create the plot
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 6))

# Plot both lines
ax.plot(years, premium_means, 'b-o', label='Premium Received')
ax.plot(years, charges_means, 'r-o', label='Total Charges Paid')

# Customize the plot
ax.set_xlabel('Year')
ax.set_ylabel('Amount per Capita')
ax.set_title('WLFR Premium Received and Total Charges Paid per Capita (2000-2023)')
ax.legend()
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

# Rotate x-axis labels for better readability
plt.xticks(years, rotation=45)

# Adjust layout to prevent label cutoff
plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()


```

Here is ignore 1999,2002 and 2004 for the same graph. 

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd

# Path to the file
path = r"C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/Final"

# Read all sheets from the Excel file
excel_file = pd.ExcelFile(f"{path}/processed_combined_data_1999-2023.xlsx")

# Initialize lists to store results
years = [year for year in range(2000, 2024) if year not in [2002, 2004]]
premium_means = []
charges_means = []

# Loop through all sheets and collect means, skipping 1999, 2002, and 2004
for sheet_name in excel_file.sheet_names[1:]:  # Start from the second sheet (2000)
    year = int(sheet_name.split('_')[1])  # Extract year from 'Year_YYYY' format
    if year in [2002, 2004]:
        continue
    
    df = pd.read_excel(excel_file, sheet_name=sheet_name)
    
    if 'WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA' in df.columns:
        premium_means.append(df['WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA'].mean())
    
    if 'WLFR_TOT_CHARGES_PAID_AMT_PER_CAPITA' in df.columns:
        charges_means.append(df['WLFR_TOT_CHARGES_PAID_AMT_PER_CAPITA'].mean())

# Create the plot
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 6))

# Plot both lines
ax.plot(years, premium_means, 'b-o', label='Premium Received')
ax.plot(years, charges_means, 'r-o', label='Total Charges Paid')

# Customize the plot
ax.set_xlabel('Year')
ax.set_ylabel('Amount per Capita')
ax.set_title('WLFR Premium Received and Total Charges Paid per Capita (2000-2023, excluding 2002 and 2004)')
ax.legend()
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

# Rotate x-axis labels for better readability
plt.xticks(years, rotation=45)

# Adjust layout to prevent label cutoff
plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()

```

This is the same plot as above but with 1% outliers removed and only 1999 removed 

```{python}

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# Path to the file
path = r"C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/Final"

# Read all sheets from the Excel file
excel_file = pd.ExcelFile(f"{path}/processed_combined_data_1999-2023.xlsx")

# Initialize lists to store results
years = []
premium_means = []
charges_means = []

# Function to remove outliers
def remove_outliers(data, threshold=2.576):  # 2.576 corresponds to 99% confidence level
    Q1 = np.percentile(data, 25)
    Q3 = np.percentile(data, 75)
    IQR = Q3 - Q1
    lower_bound = Q1 - threshold * IQR
    upper_bound = Q3 + threshold * IQR
    return [x for x in data if lower_bound <= x <= upper_bound]

# Loop through all sheets and collect means, skipping 1999
for sheet_name in excel_file.sheet_names[1:]:  # Start from the second sheet (2000)
    year = int(sheet_name.split('_')[1])  # Extract year from 'Year_YYYY' format
    
    df = pd.read_excel(excel_file, sheet_name=sheet_name)
    
    if 'WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA' in df.columns and 'WLFR_TOT_CHARGES_PAID_AMT_PER_CAPITA' in df.columns:
        premium_mean = df['WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA'].mean()
        charges_mean = df['WLFR_TOT_CHARGES_PAID_AMT_PER_CAPITA'].mean()
        
        years.append(year)
        premium_means.append(premium_mean)
        charges_means.append(charges_mean)

# Remove outliers
premium_means_clean = remove_outliers(premium_means)
charges_means_clean = remove_outliers(charges_means)

# Create a list of tuples with (year, premium, charge)
data_tuples = list(zip(years, premium_means, charges_means))

# Filter the data to keep only non-outlier points
filtered_data = [
    (year, premium, charge) for year, premium, charge in data_tuples
    if premium in premium_means_clean and charge in charges_means_clean
]

# Unzip the filtered data
years_clean, premium_means_clean, charges_means_clean = zip(*filtered_data)

# Create the plot
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 6))

# Plot both lines
ax.plot(years_clean, premium_means_clean, 'b-o', label='Premium Received')
ax.plot(years_clean, charges_means_clean, 'r-o', label='Total Charges Paid')

# Customize the plot
ax.set_xlabel('Year')
ax.set_ylabel('Amount per Capita')
ax.set_title('WLFR Premium Received and Total Charges Paid per Capita (2000-2023, outliers removed)')
ax.legend()
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

# Rotate x-axis labels for better readability
plt.xticks(years_clean, rotation=45)

# Adjust layout to prevent label cutoff
plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()


```

1% outliers removed, but keep all years

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from scipy import stats

# Path to the file
path = r"C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/Final"

# Read all sheets from the Excel file
excel_file = pd.ExcelFile(f"{path}/processed_combined_data_1999-2023.xlsx")

# Initialize lists to store results
data = []

# First, collect all data in a DataFrame
for sheet_name in excel_file.sheet_names[1:]:  # Start from the second sheet (2000)
    year = int(sheet_name.split('_')[1])
    df = pd.read_excel(excel_file, sheet_name=sheet_name)
    
    if 'WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA' in df.columns and 'WLFR_TOT_CHARGES_PAID_AMT_PER_CAPITA' in df.columns:
        data.append({
            'Year': year,
            'Premium': df['WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA'].mean(),
            'Charges': df['WLFR_TOT_CHARGES_PAID_AMT_PER_CAPITA'].mean()
        })

# Convert to DataFrame
df_annual = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Sort by year
df_annual = df_annual.sort_values('Year')

# Calculate z-scores for the entire time series
z_premium = np.abs(stats.zscore(df_annual['Premium']))
z_charges = np.abs(stats.zscore(df_annual['Charges']))

# Create mask for non-outlier data (99% confidence = z-score < 2.576)
mask = (z_premium < 2.576) & (z_charges < 2.576)

# Apply the mask
df_filtered = df_annual[mask].copy()

# Create the plot
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 6))

# Plot both lines
ax.plot(df_filtered['Year'], df_filtered['Premium'], 'b-o', label='Premium Received')
ax.plot(df_filtered['Year'], df_filtered['Charges'], 'r-o', label='Total Charges Paid')

# Customize the plot
ax.set_xlabel('Year')
ax.set_ylabel('Amount per Capita')
ax.set_title('WLFR Premium Received and Total Charges Paid per Capita\n(2000-2023, 1% outliers removed)')
ax.legend()
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

# Set x-axis ticks for all years
plt.xticks(df_filtered['Year'], rotation=45)

# Adjust layout to prevent label cutoff
plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()

```

Parallel Trends without 1999,2000, 2004

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Path to the file
path = r"C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/Final"

# Define ACA and non-ACA states
aca_states = ['AK', 'AZ', 'AR', 'CA', 'CO', 'CT', 'DE', 'DC', 'HI', 'IL', 
              'IN', 'IA', 'KY', 'LA', 'ME', 'MD', 'MA', 'MI', 'MN', 'MS', 
              'MO', 'MT', 'NE', 'NV', 'NH', 'NJ', 'NM', 'NY', 'NC', 'ND', 
              'OH', 'OK', 'OR', 'PA', 'RI', 'SC', 'SD', 'UT', 'VT', 'VA', 'WA']

non_aca_states = ['AL', 'FL', 'GA', 'ID', 'KS', 'TN', 'TX', 'WI', 'WY']

# Initialize lists to store results
years = [year for year in range(2001, 2024) if year != 2004]
aca_means = []
non_aca_means = []

# Read the Excel file
excel_file = pd.ExcelFile(f"{path}/processed_combined_data_1999-2023.xlsx")

# Loop through all sheets except 1999, 2000, and 2004
for year in years:
    sheet_name = f"Year_{year}"
    df = pd.read_excel(excel_file, sheet_name=sheet_name)
    
    # Determine which state column to use based on the year
    if year < 2009:
        state_column = 'SPONS_DFE_STATE'
    else:
        state_column = 'SPONS_DFE_MAIL_US_STATE'
    
    # Check if required columns exist
    if state_column not in df.columns:
        print(f"Warning: {state_column} column not found in sheet {sheet_name}")
        aca_means.append(np.nan)
        non_aca_means.append(np.nan)
        continue
        
    if 'WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA' not in df.columns:
        print(f"Warning: WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA column not found in sheet {sheet_name}")
        aca_means.append(np.nan)
        non_aca_means.append(np.nan)
        continue

    # Calculate means for ACA and non-ACA states
    aca_mean = df[df[state_column].isin(aca_states)]['WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA'].mean()
    non_aca_mean = df[df[state_column].isin(non_aca_states)]['WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA'].mean()
    
    aca_means.append(aca_mean)
    non_aca_means.append(non_aca_mean)

# Create the plot
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))

# Plot lines for ACA and non-ACA states
plt.plot(years, aca_means, 'b-', label='ACA States', linewidth=2)
plt.plot(years, non_aca_means, 'r-', label='Non-ACA States', linewidth=2)

# Add vertical line for ACA implementation
plt.axvline(x=2010, color='gray', linestyle='--', label='ACA Implementation')

# Customize the plot
plt.xlabel('Year')
plt.ylabel('Premium per Capita (USD)')
plt.title('Average Premium per Capita: ACA vs Non-ACA States (2001-2023, excluding 2004)')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

# Rotate x-axis labels for better readability
plt.xticks(years, rotation=45)

# Add tight layout to prevent label cutoff
plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()

```

Box plot showing post ACA premims for States with it vs without it 

```{python}

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

# Path to the file
path = r"C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/Final"

# Define ACA and non-ACA states
aca_states = ['AK', 'AZ', 'AR', 'CA', 'CO', 'CT', 'DE', 'DC', 'HI', 'IL', 
              'IN', 'IA', 'KY', 'LA', 'ME', 'MD', 'MA', 'MI', 'MN', 'MS', 
              'MO', 'MT', 'NE', 'NV', 'NH', 'NJ', 'NM', 'NY', 'NC', 'ND', 
              'OH', 'OK', 'OR', 'PA', 'RI', 'SC', 'SD', 'UT', 'VT', 'VA', 'WA']

non_aca_states = ['AL', 'FL', 'GA', 'ID', 'KS', 'TN', 'TX', 'WI', 'WY']

# Initialize lists to store data
all_data = []

# Read and process each year's data (post-ACA only, i.e., 2011 onwards)
for year in range(2011, 2024):  # Post-ACA starts from 2011
    sheet_name = f"Year_{year}"
    df = pd.read_excel(f"{path}/processed_combined_data_1999-2023.xlsx", 
                      sheet_name=sheet_name)
    
    # Determine which state column to use based on the year
    state_col = 'SPONS_DFE_MAIL_US_STATE' if year >= 2009 else 'SPONS_DFE_STATE'
    
    # Add ACA status (categorize states into ACA and Non-ACA)
    df['ACA_Status'] = df[state_col].apply(lambda x: 'ACA States' if x in aca_states else 
                                           ('Non-ACA States' if x in non_aca_states else None))
    
    # Filter out rows where ACA_Status is None (in case there are states not in either list)
    df = df[df['ACA_Status'].notna()]
    
    # Select relevant columns and add year information
    temp_df = df[['WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA', 'ACA_Status']].copy()
    temp_df['Year'] = year
    
    all_data.append(temp_df)

# Combine all data into a single DataFrame
combined_df = pd.concat(all_data)

# Remove extreme outliers for better visualization (optional)
# Using 1st and 99th percentiles as cutoffs
lower_bound = combined_df['WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA'].quantile(0.01)
upper_bound = combined_df['WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA'].quantile(0.99)
filtered_df = combined_df[
    (combined_df['WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA'] >= lower_bound) & 
    (combined_df['WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA'] <= upper_bound)
]

# Create the box plot for post-ACA period only (2011 onwards)
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
sns.boxplot(x='Year', y='WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA',
            hue='ACA_Status', data=filtered_df)

# Customize the plot
plt.title('Distribution of Premiums per Capita: Post ACA Implementation (2011-2023)')
plt.xlabel('Year')
plt.ylabel('Premium per Capita (USD)')

# Rotate x-axis labels for better readability
plt.xticks(rotation=45)

# Add grid for better readability
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

# Adjust layout to prevent label cutoff
plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()


```

For whole period not just for year by year of box plot of postACA period 

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

# Path to the file
path = r"C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/Final"

# Define ACA and non-ACA states
aca_states = ['AK', 'AZ', 'AR', 'CA', 'CO', 'CT', 'DE', 'DC', 'HI', 'IL', 
              'IN', 'IA', 'KY', 'LA', 'ME', 'MD', 'MA', 'MI', 'MN', 'MS', 
              'MO', 'MT', 'NE', 'NV', 'NH', 'NJ', 'NM', 'NY', 'NC', 'ND', 
              'OH', 'OK', 'OR', 'PA', 'RI', 'SC', 'SD', 'UT', 'VT', 'VA', 'WA']

non_aca_states = ['AL', 'FL', 'GA', 'ID', 'KS', 'TN', 'TX', 'WI', 'WY']

# Initialize lists to store data
all_data = []

# Read and process each year's data (post-ACA only)
for year in range(2011, 2024):
    sheet_name = f"Year_{year}"
    df = pd.read_excel(f"{path}/processed_combined_data_1999-2023.xlsx", 
                      sheet_name=sheet_name)
    
    state_col = 'SPONS_DFE_MAIL_US_STATE'
    
    # Add ACA status
    df['ACA_Status'] = df[state_col].apply(lambda x: 'ACA States' if x in aca_states else 
                                           ('Non-ACA States' if x in non_aca_states else None))
    
    # Filter out rows where ACA_Status is None
    df = df[df['ACA_Status'].notna()]
    
    # Select relevant columns
    temp_df = df[['WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA', 'ACA_Status']].copy()
    
    all_data.append(temp_df)

# Combine all data
combined_df = pd.concat(all_data)

# Remove extreme outliers
lower_bound = combined_df['WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA'].quantile(0.01)
upper_bound = combined_df['WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA'].quantile(0.99)
filtered_df = combined_df[
    (combined_df['WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA'] >= lower_bound) & 
    (combined_df['WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA'] <= upper_bound)
]

# Create the box plot
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.boxplot(x='ACA_Status', y='WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA', 
            data=filtered_df, 
            palette=['#d62728', '#1f77b4'])  # Blue for ACA, Red for Non-ACA 

# Customize the plot
plt.title('Distribution of Premiums per Capita: Post-ACA Implementation (2011-2023)')
plt.xlabel('State Category')
plt.ylabel('Premium per Capita (USD)')

# Add grid for better readability
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

# Adjust layout
plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()

```


Regression + Controls

Controls Variables 
Year = Year
Inflation
Inflation_annual_change
S&P_Yearly_Growth
Federal_Funds_Rate_Average_Yield


```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import statsmodels.api as sm
from statsmodels.stats.outliers_influence import variance_inflation_factor

# Path to the file
path = r"C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/Final"

# Define ACA and non-ACA states
aca_states = ['AK', 'AZ', 'AR', 'CA', 'CO', 'CT', 'DE', 'DC', 'HI', 'IL', 
              'IN', 'IA', 'KY', 'LA', 'ME', 'MD', 'MA', 'MI', 'MN', 'MS', 
              'MO', 'MT', 'NE', 'NV', 'NH', 'NJ', 'NM', 'NY', 'NC', 'ND', 
              'OH', 'OK', 'OR', 'PA', 'RI', 'SC', 'SD', 'UT', 'VT', 'VA', 'WA']

non_aca_states = ['AL', 'FL', 'GA', 'ID', 'KS', 'TN', 'TX', 'WI', 'WY']

# Initialize lists to store data
all_data = []

# Read control variables
controls_data = pd.read_excel(f"{path}/Controls_Dataset_2.xlsx")

# Read and process main data
excel_file = pd.ExcelFile(f"{path}/processed_combined_data_1999-2023.xlsx")

# Process each year's data (excluding 1999, 2000, and 2004)
for year in range(2001, 2024):
    if year != 2004:  # Skip 2004
        try:
            # Read sheet
            df = pd.read_excel(excel_file, sheet_name=f"Year_{year}")
            
            # Determine state column
            state_col = 'SPONS_DFE_MAIL_US_STATE' if year >= 2009 else 'SPONS_DFE_STATE'
            
            # Create ACA dummy
            df['ACA_State'] = df[state_col].isin(aca_states).astype(int)
            
            # Create post-treatment dummy (2011 onwards for post-ACA)
            df['Post_ACA'] = np.where(year >= 2011, 1, 0)
            
            # Create interaction term
            df['ACA_x_Post'] = df['ACA_State'] * df['Post_ACA']
            
            # Add year
            df['Year'] = year
            
            # Keep relevant columns and remove rows with missing values
            cols_to_keep = ['Year', 'ACA_State', 'Post_ACA', 'ACA_x_Post',
                           'WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA',
                           'WLFR_TOT_CHARGES_PAID_AMT_PER_CAPITA']
            
            df = df[cols_to_keep].dropna()
            
            # Remove zeros from dependent variables
            df = df[df['WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA'] > 0]
            df = df[df['WLFR_TOT_CHARGES_PAID_AMT_PER_CAPITA'] > 0]
            
            all_data.append(df)
            
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error processing year {year}: {str(e)}")
            continue

# Combine all years
panel_data = pd.concat(all_data, ignore_index=True)

# Merge with controls data
panel_data = panel_data.merge(controls_data[['Year', 'Inflation', 'Inflation_annual_change', 
                                           'S&P_Yearly_Growth', 'Federal_Funds_Rate_Average_Yield']], 
                             on='Year', how='left')

# Log transform the dependent variables
panel_data['Log_Premium'] = np.log(panel_data['WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA'])
panel_data['Log_Charges'] = np.log(panel_data['WLFR_TOT_CHARGES_PAID_AMT_PER_CAPITA'])

# Print data diagnostics
print("\nData Shape:", panel_data.shape)
print("\nMissing Values:\n", panel_data.isnull().sum())
print("\nSummary Statistics:\n", panel_data.describe())

# Run DiD regression for Premium Received (Experience-Rated Plans)
Y1 = panel_data['Log_Premium']
X1 = panel_data[['ACA_State', 'Post_ACA', 'ACA_x_Post', 
                 'Inflation', 'Inflation_annual_change', 
                 'S&P_Yearly_Growth', 'Federal_Funds_Rate_Average_Yield']]
X1 = sm.add_constant(X1)

model1 = sm.OLS(Y1, X1)
results1 = model1.fit(cov_type='HC3')  # Using robust standard errors

# Run DiD regression for Total Charges Paid (Non-Experience-Rated Plans)
Y2 = panel_data['Log_Charges']
X2 = X1  # Same independent variables

model2 = sm.OLS(Y2, X2)
results2 = model2.fit(cov_type='HC3')  # Using robust standard errors

# Create summary DataFrames
def create_results_df(results):
    return pd.DataFrame({
        'Coefficient': results.params,
        'Std Error': results.bse,
        't-stat': results.tvalues,
        'p-value': results.pvalues,
        'CI Lower': results.conf_int()[0],
        'CI Upper': results.conf_int()[1]
    })

# Create results DataFrames
results_df1 = create_results_df(results1)
results_df2 = create_results_df(results2)

# Print results
print("\nResults for Premium Received (Experience-Rated Plans):")
print(results_df1)
print("\nR-squared:", results1.rsquared)
print("Adjusted R-squared:", results1.rsquared_adj)
print("\nF-statistic:", results1.fvalue)
print("Prob (F-statistic):", results1.f_pvalue)

print("\nResults for Total Charges Paid (Non-Experience-Rated Plans):")
print(results_df2)
print("\nR-squared:", results2.rsquared)
print("Adjusted R-squared:", results2.rsquared_adj)
print("\nF-statistic:", results2.fvalue)
print("Prob (F-statistic):", results2.f_pvalue)

# Save results to Excel
with pd.ExcelWriter(f"{path}/regression_results_with_controls_logged.xlsx") as writer:
    results_df1.to_excel(writer, sheet_name='Premium_Received_Results')
    results_df2.to_excel(writer, sheet_name='Total_Charges_Results')

```
